elements.text: (l) Earnest money deposit (EMD)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When an EMD for a purchase transaction Mortgage is used to qualify the Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must document that the EMD cleared the Borrower’s account (e.g., copy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled check, asset account statement, written statement from the EMD holder verifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt of the funds or wire transfer confirmation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the EMD is needed to meet the minimum contribution from Borrower personal funds,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verify that the source of the EMD is an eligible asset type and document it in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the applicable requirements in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide account statement(s) (based on Streamlined Accept or Standard documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, as applicable) or a direct account verification (i.e., verification of deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (VOD)) that covers the period up to and including the date the EMD funds cleared the
elements.label: text

elements.text: account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The EMD must not be counted twice in the evaluation of the Mortgage (i.e., deducted from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the funds to close and counted in assets).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the source of the Borrower’s EMD is gift funds, see Section 5501.4(a)(1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m)Business assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funds from a Borrower’s business account may be used to qualify the Borrower, provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: they meet the requirements of this chapter, except as stated below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation of large deposits, as described in Section 5501.1(f), is not required provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Seller:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reviews a minimum of the most recent two months of the business account statements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determines the deposits are typical for the Borrower’s business
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5304.1(l) for additional requirements when self-employed income from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: business is used for qualifying.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) Source of funds from outside the United States and its territories
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the source of funds needed for closing is, or otherwise originates from, asset(s) located
elements.label: text

elements.text: outside the United States and its territories:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funds must be transferred into a U.S.- or State-regulated financial institution and verified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in U.S. dollars prior to the closing of the Mortgage transaction, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Combined value of the assets must be at least 20% greater than the amount from these
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets needed for closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5102.3(b) for additional requirements when funds from outside the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: and its territories are used to qualify the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (o) Cryptocurrency
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any amount of cryptocurrency must be exchanged for U.S. dollars if it will be used as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: source of funds for the Mortgage transaction (i.e., any funds required to be paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and Borrower reserves).

elements.text: 5501.6: Interested party contributions (09/03/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interested party contributions: definitions, requirements, exceptions and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financing concessions: definition, acceptable uses and maximum limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales concessions: definition and impact on loan-to-value (LTV) calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Interested party contributions: definitions, requirements, exceptions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Mortgages that include interested party contributions under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the Purchase Documents and this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Interested party definition
elements.label: text

elements.text: An interested party is any person or entity that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May benefit from the property selling at the highest possible price, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Can influence the sales price or other terms of the real estate transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are always considered interested parties:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Builder
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Developer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real estate agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real estate agent’s broker and agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any contributing party (e.g., Seller, originating lender, employer, municipality,
elements.label: text

elements.text: nonprofit organization or Related Person) affiliated with any of the above individuals
elements.label: text

elements.text: or entities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this section, an affiliation exists when the two parties are owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: controlled by a common third party or when one of the parties has ownership or control
elements.label: text

elements.text: over the other.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Interested party contributions requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interested party contributions are any contributions that are made:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ By an interested party, as defined above,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In connection with the Mortgage or the real estate transaction,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To or on behalf of the Borrower, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Directly or indirectly through a third party, including a donation to a nonprofit entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Mortgages with interested party contributions that are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financing concessions meeting the requirements in Section 5501.6(b) below, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales concessions meeting the requirements in Section 5501.6(c) below
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Exceptions to interested party contribution requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following types of contributions are not subject to the interested party contributions
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender credit: A lender credit provided by an originating lender affiliated with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: interested party to the transaction, provided the lender credit is derived from an
elements.label: text

elements.text: increase in the interest rate (i.e., premium pricing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gifts from a Related Person: Gift funds or gift of equity from a Related Person who
elements.label: text

elements.text: is also the seller of the subject property, provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  The donor is not the builder or another interested party and has no affiliation with
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other interested party to the transaction, and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  All of the requirements for gift funds or gift of equity from a Related Person as
elements.label: text

elements.text: stated in Section 5501.4(a) are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real estate tax credits: Prorated real estate tax credits contributed by the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: seller in areas where real estate taxes are paid in arrears
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Builder forward commitment costs: The costs associated with a forward
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitment obtained by a builder from a lender in order to offer future buyers a
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific Mortgage interest rate, provided the forward commitment was obtained prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to entering into a contract with the Borrower and it was not obtained specifically for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Seller must deliver ULDD Data Point Investor Feature Identifier valid
elements.label: text

elements.text: value “J70” for each Mortgage with rates provided based on builder forward
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitments as required by Section 6302.53.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Documentation of interested party contributions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount and the source of all interested party contributions must be documented in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file and be clearly shown on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Financing concessions: definition, acceptable uses and maximum limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Definition and acceptable uses of financing concessions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing concessions are interested party contributions that may only be used to pay:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s Closing Costs, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Up to 12 months of homeowners association (HOA) dues, provided that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement shows that:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  The funds for the payment of the HOA dues were collected at closing, and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  The funds were disbursed directly to the HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contributions exceeding the amount of the Borrower’s actual Closing Costs and up to 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months’ HOA dues (if applicable), as stated above, must meet the requirements for sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions in Section 5501.6(c) below, except that the payment of more than 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months’ HOA dues is considered an abatement as described below in 5501.6(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Maximum financing concessions limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum permitted financing concessions, calculated as a percentage of value as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 4203.1(a), are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maximum financing concession limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Occupancy LTV/total LTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: (TLTV) ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: LTV/TLTV ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: LTV/TLTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratios ≤ 75%
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residences
elements.label: text

elements.text: and second
elements.label: text

elements.text: homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions: The maximum financing concession limits above do not apply to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financing concessions contributed by Freddie Mac as the property seller for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originated for the purchase of Freddie Mac REO properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower fees or costs customarily paid by the property seller according to local
elements.label: text

elements.text: convention
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amount of any financing concessions exceeding the limits stated above must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements for sales concessions in Section 5501.6(c) below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Sales concessions: definition and impact on LTV calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Sales concessions definition
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sales concessions are interested party contributions, that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exceed the maximum financing concessions percentage limitations in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5501.6(b)(ii) above, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Do not meet the acceptable uses of financing concession in 5501.6(b)(i) above, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Cash or cash-like contributions (e.g., a gift card), decorator allowances, vacations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: furniture, automobiles, securities or other giveaways
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Rebates (e.g., rebates from real estate agents) that are not financing concessions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reimbursement to the Borrower for payment of fees charged to process or
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiate a short sale (commonly referred to as short sale processing fees, short
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale negotiation fees, buyer discount fees or short sale buyer fees)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Impact of sales concessions on LTV calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor will determine the LTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio based on the purchase price or appraised value of the Mortgaged Premises and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: dollar amount or value of sales concessions entered into Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manually Underwritten Mortgages, when calculating the LTV ratio:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For purposes of determining the value of the Mortgaged Premises pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4203.1(a), the dollar amount or value of any sales concessions must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: deducted from the purchase price
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use the lower of the reduced purchase price (after the reduction for all sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions has been made) or the appraised value of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ineligible Mortgages include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with interested party contributions paid outside of closing and not disclosed
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with abatements (that are funds provided to a lender or third party by an
elements.label: text

elements.text: interested party to pay or reimburse in whole or in part a certain number of monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments of principal, interest, taxes, insurance and/or other assessments on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s behalf in excess of Prepaid/Escrows associated with the Mortgage closing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: including payment of more than 12 months of HOA dues.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5501.7: Lender contributions (09/03/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender credit: definition and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender incentive: definition and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Lender credit: definition and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender credit is a contribution by the originating lender toward the Borrower’s Closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Costs. Lender credit must meet all of the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of lender credit must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be derived from an increase in the interest rate (i.e., premium pricing), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be funded directly by the lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender credit must not require repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third party funds must not be used to provide a lender credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender credit may only be used as a credit towards the Borrower’s Closing Costs. In the
elements.label: text

elements.text: event the lender credit exceeds the amount of the Borrower’s Closing Costs, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The lender credit must be reduced so it does not exceed the amount of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Closing Costs, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The amount of the lender credit that exceeds the Borrower’s Closing Costs must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: applied as a principal curtailment to the Mortgage and must be clearly reflected on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement. (See Section 6302.32 for delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Mortgages with principal curtailments.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lender credit derived from an increase in the interest rate (i.e., premium pricing) must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be used as a credit towards funding a temporary subsidy buydown plan on a “no cash￾out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Lender incentive: definition and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A lender incentive is a cash or a cash-like contribution (e.g., a gift card), or other item of
elements.label: text

elements.text: value that is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provided by the originating lender to the Borrower, directly or through a third party, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not a lender credit toward the Borrower’s Closing Costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with lender incentives, as described above, are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if
elements.label: text

elements.text: they meet the following requirements, regardless of whether the incentive is provided before,
elements.label: text

elements.text: on or after the Note Date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ No repayment of the incentive may be required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The cost or value of the incentive must not be funded through the Mortgage transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., premium pricing)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The incentive must not be considered when qualifying the Borrower (e.g., as a source of
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds for closing or reserves)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lender incentive must be treated as a sales concession as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5501.6(c) if the originating lender is, or is affiliated with, an interested party to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A lender incentive is not considered cash out to the Borrower and does not have to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the calculation of the Mortgage proceeds, including the calculation of cash back
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5501.8: Automated asset assessment with Loan
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Product Advisor® using account data (09/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible and ineligible asset types and other sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submission requirements, representation and warranty relief eligibility and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Representation and warranty relief eligibility is contingent on the Seller’s compliance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Asset and income modeler (AIM) automated asset assessment using account data provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers with the option to use Loan Product Advisor® to determine whether a Seller is eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for relief from enforcement of certain representations and warranties related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s assets.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain the Borrower’s account data and submit all required data and
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to Loan Product Advisor. Based on the data submitted, Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: will assess for representation and warranty relief eligibility and return the results of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible and ineligible asset types and other sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table describes eligible and ineligible asset types and other sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: for an automated asset assessment using account data:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible and ineligible asset types and other sources of funds for an automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: asset assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible asset type Ineligible sources of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following asset types as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5501.3:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Depository accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s business checking, savings
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or money market accounts. (See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5304.1(l) for additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Securities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retirement accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must not be a Mortgage for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which any of the following are being used
elements.label: text

elements.text: to qualify the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash on hand
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assets that will be used by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for repayment of Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly obligations as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5307.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following other eligible sources of
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds as described in Section 5501.4:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gift funds or a gift of equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gift funds received as a wedding gift
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gift funds received as a graduation gift
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A gift or grant from an Agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each verification report obtained, the Seller must confirm:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each asset on the verification report is owned by at least one Borrower and such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has access to the funds in the asset account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All assets shown on the verification report are in U.S. dollars located in a U.S.- or
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Age of documentation

elements.text: on the Last Feedback Certificate complies with the requirements in Section 5102.4.

elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Data submission requirements

elements.text: Seller must:

elements.text: ■ Submit the most current account data to Loan Product Advisor. If, after the initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission, the Seller obtains an updated verification report, the Seller must resubmit
elements.label: text

elements.text: the account data to Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investigate and resolve any inconsistent or contradictory information between the
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification report, information contained in Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Application (including the Borrower’s asset representations), and the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: and, if applicable, resubmit to Loan Product Advisor with the correct information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verify any funds required for the Mortgage transaction that are not included in the
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: to bring funds to the closing) and maintain the documentation in the Mortgage file

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: section.

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects the Seller to place as much emphasis on the adequacy of the property as
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral as it does on underwriting the Borrower’s creditworthiness. The conclusion that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac must be based on the determination that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is creditworthy (acceptable credit reputation and capacity) and the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is adequate and acceptable collateral for the Mortgage transaction. The Seller is
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for determining the eligibility of the property and the acceptability of the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase eligible Mortgages secured by residential properties in urban,
elements.label: text

elements.text: suburban and rural Market Areas provided the Mortgaged Premises is adequate collateral for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage transaction based on the value, condition and marketability of the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be located in a State
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be primarily residential in nature based on the characteristics of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be an attached, semi detached or detached dwelling unit(s) located on an individual lot,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a Planned Unit Development (PUD), in a Condominium Project or in a Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project. See Section 5601.6 for eligibility of a property with two or more adjoining
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcels. See Chapter 5701 for special requirements for condominiums. See Chapter 5705
elements.label: text

elements.text: for special requirements for cooperatives.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be safe, sound and structurally secure. See Section 5605.5 for acceptable overall
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition ratings and indications of unacceptable collateral conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be complete or comply with the conditions in Section 5601.3 for eligible incomplete
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Represent the highest and best use of the property as improved (or as proposed per plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: and specifications)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an eligible zoning compliance in accordance with Section 5605.4(a)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have legal access (ingress and egress) (see Section 5605.4(a)(iv))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be suitable for year-round occupancy regardless of the location, except as specifically
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted otherwise in Section 4201.12 for certain second homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have utilities that meet community standards (see Section 5605.4(a)(iii))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have mechanical systems that meet community standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have adequate property insurance coverage that meets Freddie Mac’s requirements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including coverage for hazards specific to the location of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be subject to a pending legal proceeding for condemnation in whole or in part
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not purchase Mortgages secured by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Vacant land, undeveloped land or land development properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties used primarily for agriculture or farming
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties used primarily for commercial enterprises (including, but not limited to, bed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and breakfasts, boarding houses, Condominium Hotels and units located in a PUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: operating as a hotel or similar type of transient housing that includes hotel type services
elements.label: text

elements.text: and characteristics)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A property being used as a group home for individuals with disabilities is not considered an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ineligible property. A Mortgage secured by such a property may be eligible for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, provided it meets the requirements of the Guide, including but not limited to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the residential requirements described above in Section 5601.1(a) and the occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements identified in Sections 4201.11 and 4201.13.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5701.3 for a list of ineligible condominium project types and Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.3 for a list of ineligible cooperative project types.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.2: Eligibility of a property with an ADU (02/09/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible properties with ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible properties with ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured Home ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible properties with ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase an eligible Mortgage secured by a 1-, 2- or 3-unit property that
elements.label: text

elements.text: has one ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes, including CHOICEHomes®, with an ADU,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the primary dwelling unit must be a multiwide Manufactured Home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ADU is classified as an additional finished area that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Includes a kitchen, bathroom and separate entrance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The absence of cooking appliance(s) in a kitchen does not change the classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: as an ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is independent of the primary dwelling unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is smaller in size than the primary dwelling unit (i.e., the ADU’s finished area is less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the primary dwelling unit’s finished area) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contributes less to the value of the property than the primary dwelling unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of eligible ADU configurations include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 1-, 2-, or 3-unit dwelling with an ADU above a garage or outbuilding,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A dwelling with an attached or detached ADU, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A dwelling with an ADU in the basement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible properties with ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by the following ADU configurations are not eligible for purchase by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 2- or 3-unit property with an ADU that does not comply with zoning and land use
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements (illegal zoning)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 1-, 2- or 3-unit property with two or more ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 4-unit property with an ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Appraisal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must determine whether the subject property has an ADU and report the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal on the appropriate form. When determining whether the subject property has an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ADU, the appraiser must consider zoning and land use requirements and all property
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics, specifically the unit’s utility and the property’s highest and best use.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the appraiser determines the subject property has an ADU, the appraisal report must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A detailed description of the ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any effect the ADU has on the market value or marketability of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specific information about the ADU, including but not limited to its general condition, its
elements.label: text

elements.text: room count (including the number of bedrooms and baths) and the finished square
elements.label: text

elements.text: footage area
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) For properties with legal or legal non-conforming zoning compliance or locations
elements.label: text

elements.text: without zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject property ADU complies with the zoning and land use requirements (legal or
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal non-conforming or locations without zoning), the appraisal report must include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one comparable sale with an ADU, when available, is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrate the property’s conformity and marketability to its Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If a recent comparable sale with an ADU is not available in the subject’s Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area, the appraiser can use an older sale with an ADU from the subject’s Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area or a sale with an ADU from a competing Market Area as a comparable sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: or as supporting market data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The appraiser may always use more than three comparable sales, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract sales (pending sales) and/or current listings, to justify and support his or
elements.label: text

elements.text: her adjustments and opinion of market value, as long as at least three are actual
elements.label: text

elements.text: closed (settled) sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a comparable sale with an ADU is not available, the appraiser can use a comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale in the subject’s Market Area without an ADU as long as the appraiser can justify
elements.label: text

elements.text: and support such use in the appraisal report. Freddie Mac will purchase eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by a property with an ADU if the appraiser can develop a credible
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinion of market value for the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable requirements for properties with illegal zoning compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject property ADU does not comply with the zoning and land use requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (illegal zoning), the Mortgage is eligible if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subject property is a 1-unit property with an ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal contains at least two comparable sales with an ADU that is non￾compliant with the zoning and land use requirements (illegal zoning) to demonstrate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the marketability of the subject property to its Market Area; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller confirms that the existence of the ADU will not jeopardize future hazard
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: 2- or 3-unit properties with an ADU that do not comply with the zoning and land
elements.label: text

elements.text: use requirements (illegal zoning) are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Manufactured Home ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase a Mortgage secured by a 1-, 2-, or 3-unit property that contains a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home ADU that meets the following additional requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home ADU must be legally classified as real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home ADU must have a minimum of 400 square feet of finished area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home ADU must meet the general requirements related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes in Section 5703.2, property eligibility requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.3(a) and titling and lien requirements in Section 5703.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes, including CHOICEHomes, containing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ADU, the primary dwelling unit must be a multiwide Manufactured Home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the appraiser determines the subject property has an ADU, the appraisal report must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A detailed description of the ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any effect the ADU has on the market value or marketability of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specific information about the ADU, including, but not limited to, its location within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling, its interior condition, its room summary (e.g., bathrooms, bedrooms, kitchen)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the above-grade and below-grade finished and unfinished area
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) For properties with legal or legal non-conforming zoning compliance or locations
elements.label: text

elements.text: without zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject property ADU complies with the zoning and land use requirements (legal or
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal non-conforming or locations without zoning), the appraisal report must include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one comparable sale with an ADU, when available, is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrate the property’s conformity and marketability to its Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If a recent comparable sale with an ADU is not available in the subject property’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area, the appraisal report can include an older sale with an ADU from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property’s Market Area or a sale with an ADU from a competing Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area as a comparable sale or as supporting market data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The appraisal report may include more than three comparable sales, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract sales (pending sales) and/or current listings, to support the adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the opinion of market value, as long as at least three are closed (settled) sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a comparable sale with an ADU is not available, the appraisal report can include
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales in the subject property’s Market Area without an ADU as long as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal report includes justification for such use
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable requirements for properties with illegal zoning compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject property ADU does not comply with the zoning and land use requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (illegal zoning), the Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subject property is a 1-unit property with an ADU
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report includes at least two comparable sales with an ADU that is non￾compliant with the zoning and land use requirements (illegal zoning) to demonstrate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the marketability of the subject property to its Market Area; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller confirms that the existence of the ADU will not jeopardize future hazard
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Mortgage secured by a 2- or 3-unit property with an ADU that does not comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the zoning and land use requirements (illegal zoning) is not eligible for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Manufactured Home ADUs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase a Mortgage secured by a 1-, 2-, or 3-unit property that contains a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home ADU that meets the following additional requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home ADU must be legally classified as real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home ADU must have a minimum of 400 square feet of finished area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home ADU must meet the general requirements related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes in Section 5703.2, property eligibility requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.3(a) and titling and lien requirements in Section 5703.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes, including CHOICEHomes, containing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ADU, the primary dwelling unit must be a multiwide Manufactured Home.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchases Mortgages secured by properties with energy-efficient improvements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties with solar panels as specified below. This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties with energy-efficient improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties with solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Properties with energy-efficient improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify energy-efficient features (e.g., photovoltaic systems, water efficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements, energy-efficient windows) or high-performing energy-efficient homes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any impact to market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assess the contributory value of energy improvements and any premium paid for a high￾performing energy-efficient home based on the market reaction, similar to any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: property feature
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consider appropriate valuation methods (e.g., income approach, cost analysis, discounted
elements.label: text

elements.text: cash flows, or market surveys or any other applicable methods) when determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: contributory value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Justify and support any adjustments in the appraisal report in an addendum or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplementary documentation, if necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisers must be familiar with energy reports, energy ratings or other new concepts that
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be developed to identify the energy efficiency of a home. If relied upon, any reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be generally acceptable, and, if available, these reports and information must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the appraisers’ analysis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional due diligence may be necessary if the high-performing energy-efficient home or
elements.label: text

elements.text: energy improvements are new to the market and there are a lack of sales with similar features
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a lack of data available from traditional data sources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Appraisal Institute’s Form 820.05, Residential Green and Energy Efficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: Addendum, may be used to collect and report energy efficiency data associated with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resources
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller resources related to energy-efficient properties and the appraisal of properties with
elements.label: text

elements.text: energy-efficient features:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Appraisal Institute (including the use of the Residential Green and Energy Efficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: Addendum)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Home Energy Rating System (HERS®) Index provided by the Residential Energy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Services Network (RESNET®)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Home Energy Score provided by the U.S. Department of Energy’s Better
elements.label: text

elements.text: Buildings® initiative
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ EiValue® – Tool used in the valuation of photovoltaic systems and/or the energy related
elements.label: text

elements.text: efficiency of a property (income and cost approaches)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ PV Value® – Tool used in the valuation of photovoltaic systems (income approach)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Properties with solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must take into consideration ownership of the solar panels and any liens upon the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property relating to debt or lease payments used to obtain the solar panels. For example, solar
elements.label: text

elements.text: panels not owned by the Borrower can be financed via several types of agreements such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease agreements or a power purchase agreement (PPA).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must also review any UCC-1 Financing Statement or lease agreement associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the solar panels to determine if liens are against the real estate or against the solar
elements.label: text

elements.text: panels. The property must maintain access to electrical utilities consistent with community
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.4: Eligibility of properties with energy-efficient
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: improvements and properties with solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchases Mortgages secured by properties with energy-efficient improvements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties with solar panels as specified below. This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties with energy-efficient improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties with solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Properties with energy-efficient improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify energy-efficient features (e.g., photovoltaic systems, water efficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements, energy-efficient windows) or high-performing energy-efficient homes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify any impact to value or marketability
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assess the contributory value of energy improvements and any premium paid for a high￾performing energy-efficient home based on the market reaction, similar to any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: property feature
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consider appropriate valuation methods (e.g., income approach, cost analysis, discounted
elements.label: text

elements.text: cash flows, or market surveys or any other applicable methods) when determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: contributory value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Justify and support any adjustments in the appraisal report by including commentary or
elements.label: text

elements.text: exhibits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include photos of energy-efficient or green features
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify whether the property has an efficiency rating; if the property has an efficiency
elements.label: text

elements.text: rating, the rating organization, rating and score must be identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisers must be familiar with energy reports, energy ratings or other concepts that may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: developed to identify the energy efficiency of a home. If relied upon, any reports must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: generally acceptable, and, if available, these reports and information must be included in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisers’ analysis and included in the appraisal report as an exhibit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional due diligence may be necessary if the high-performing energy-efficient home or
elements.label: text

elements.text: energy improvements are new to the market and there are a lack of sales with similar features
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a lack of data available from traditional data sources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Appraisal Institute’s Form 820.05, Residential Green and Energy Efficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: Addendum, may be used to collect and report energy efficiency data associated with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: property. If Form 820.05 is used, it must be included as an exhibit in the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resources
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller resources related to energy-efficient properties and the appraisal of properties with
elements.label: text

elements.text: energy-efficient features:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Appraisal Institute (including the use of the Residential Green and Energy Efficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: Addendum)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Home Energy Rating System (HERS®) Index provided by the Residential Energy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Services Network (RESNET®)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Home Energy Score provided by the U.S. Department of Energy’s Better
elements.label: text

elements.text: Buildings® initiative
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ EiValue® – Tool used in the valuation of photovoltaic systems and/or the energy related
elements.label: text

elements.text: efficiency of a property (income and cost approaches)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ PV Value® – Tool used in the valuation of photovoltaic systems (income approach)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Properties with solar panels
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must take into consideration ownership of the solar panels and any liens upon the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property relating to debt or lease payments used to obtain the solar panels. For example, solar
elements.label: text

elements.text: panels not owned by the Borrower can be financed via several types of agreements such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease agreements or a power purchase agreement (PPA).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must also review any UCC-1 Financing Statement or lease agreement associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the solar panels to determine if liens are against the real estate or against the solar
elements.label: text

elements.text: panels. The property must maintain access to electrical utilities consistent with community
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.5: Eligibility of a property with mixed-use
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchases Mortgages secured by residential properties with mixed-use
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraiser requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A residential property with mixed-use must meet the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property must be a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property must be located in a predominantly residential Neighborhood, be primarily
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential in nature based on the characteristics of the property and be typical for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties in the Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The mixed-use must represent a legal, permissible use of the property under the local
elements.label: text

elements.text: zoning requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the property has a commercial use, the Borrower must be the owner and the operator of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the business
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The dwelling may not be modified in a manner that has an adverse impact on its
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability as a residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The commercial use must not have an adverse effect on the habitability and safety of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or site
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appraiser requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must provide the following when appraising a mixed-use property:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An appraisal with an interior and exterior inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A detailed description of any accommodations made for the commercial use of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A discussion of any positive or adverse impacts of the commercial use and whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cost to restore the property to solely residential use will affect its value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement describing any market resistance to the commercial use and adjustments for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any commercial features made to the comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An opinion of market value based on the property’s residential nature
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5701.8(b)(ii) for Condominium Unit live-work requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.5: Eligibility of a property with mixed-use (Future
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchases Mortgages secured by residential properties with mixed-use
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal report requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A residential property with mixed-use must meet the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property must be a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property must be located in a predominantly residential Neighborhood, be primarily
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential in nature based on the characteristics of the property and be typical for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties in the Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The mixed-use must represent a legal, permissible use of the property under the local
elements.label: text

elements.text: zoning requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the property has a commercial use, the Borrower must be the owner and the operator of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the business
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The dwelling may not be modified in a manner that has an adverse impact on its
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability as a residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The commercial use must not have an adverse effect on the habitability and safety of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or site
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appraisal report requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must deliver a Traditional Appraisal Report or Hybrid Appraisal Report that
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The type(s) of non-residential use observed on the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identification of whether the use is adverse, beneficial or neutral. If the use is adverse or
elements.label: text

elements.text: beneficial, a description of the impact to value or marketability is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Commentary related to whether the cost to restore the property to solely residential use
elements.label: text

elements.text: will affect its value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Commentary describing any market resistance to the commercial use
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adjustments to the comparable sales related to the commercial use
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An opinion of market value based on the property’s residential use
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5701.8(b)(ii) for Condominium Unit live-work requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.6: Eligibility of a property with two or more
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: adjoining parcels (01/26/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5605.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Mortgaged Premises includes two or more parcels of real estate, the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The parcels must be adjoining.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: Parcels that would otherwise be adjoined but are separated by a road (e.g., a
elements.label: text

elements.text: waterfront property where the parcel without the residence offers access to the water) are
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible if the parcel without the residence is non-buildable but may include non-residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements such as a garage or dock and the Seller provides evidence in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the parcel without the residence cannot be improved with a dwelling.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must contain only one residence. (An ADU is allowed only on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcel that contains the residence if the eligibility requirements of Section 5601.2 are met.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a valid First Lien on each parcel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each parcel must have the same basic zoning (e.g., residential or agricultural)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each parcel must be conveyed in its entirety
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The site description must accurately describe the land and any improvements included on
elements.label: text

elements.text: each of the parcels
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The residence or improvements may be built across the lot lines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-residential improvements such as garages or outbuildings are acceptable on any parcel
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the appraiser must consider how the existence of two or more parcels, adjoining or
elements.label: text

elements.text: not, influence the value use and marketability of the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.6: Eligibility of a property with two or more
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: adjoining parcels (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective November 2, 2026, Section 5601.6 is deleted. See Section 5605.4 for information
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to eligibility of a property with two or more adjoining parcels.

elements.text: 5601.7: Seller verification of subject property Owner of
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verifying the Owner of Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investigating and documenting Owner of Record discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Verifying the Owner of Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must verify the information outlined in the table below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Owner of Record verification by transaction type
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transaction type The Seller must verify:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase ■ The property seller listed on the sales contract is the Owner
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Record of the subject property; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the transaction involves the sale of land separate from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the dwelling, the property seller listed on the sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the land is the Owner of Record for the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refinance The Borrower is an Owner of Record of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payoff of a land
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property seller is the vendor on the recorded land
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract and the Owner of Record of the subject property;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is a vendee on the recorded land contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Investigating and documenting Owner of Record discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property seller for purchase transactions or the Borrower for refinance transactions is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not the Owner of Record, the Seller must investigate the circumstances of the transaction to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure the transaction is legitimate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller determines the transaction is legitimate, the Seller must retain in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file documentation evidencing the verification or legitimacy of the transaction, which may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraiser’s analysis and conclusions in the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A property sales history report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the recorded deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of a property tax bill
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of property title information indicating legal ownership of the property (e.g., title
elements.label: text

elements.text: property report summary, title commitment or binder, attorney opinion of title letter)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Value warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condition warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Marketability warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to all other representations and warranties specified in the Seller’s Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, the Seller makes the following representations and warranties:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Value warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that the appraisal accurately reflects the market value of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5604.3 for information regarding requirements for Mortgages with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement Dates more than 120 days after the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condition warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Settlement Date, the Seller represents and warrants that the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not in C5 or C6 condition. For property types not required to utilize the Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset condition ratings, the Seller represents that the subject property is in a condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with condition ratings C1 through C4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Marketability warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Settlement Date, the Seller represents and warrants that the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable to typical purchasers in the subject property’s Market Area.

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:

elements.text: ■ Loan Collateral Advisor® overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exemptions to appraisal underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exceptions to Loan Collateral Advisor appraised value representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan Collateral Advisor overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with appraisals submitted to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® (UCDP®)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and assessed through Loan Collateral Advisor, Freddie Mac will not exercise its remedies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the issuance of repurchase requests, in connection with a breach of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling representations and warranties related to the appraised value of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises, provided the requirements below are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements must be met for Mortgages to be eligible for appraised value
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must have a loan-to-value (LTV)/total TLTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Credit (HELOC) TLTV (HTLTV) ratio less than or equal to 95%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be secured by a 1-unit dwelling (including units in a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project or a Planned Unit Development (PUD))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must be an individual or a Living Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UCDP feedback message FRE4000 must be returned, indicating “This appraisal is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for representation and warranty relief for property value, pending an assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the loan”.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Sellers using Loan Collateral Advisor, representation and warranty relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility corresponds to an appraisal with a risk score of 2.5 or lower. Loan Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor will also display an “eligible” indicator. Upon submission to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor® and/or Loan Quality Advisor®, a corresponding representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief message will also be provided in the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The final submission to Loan Selling Advisor® must indicate the representation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranty relief status is “Y” or “Yes”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages are ineligible for appraised value representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Mortgaged Premises subject to resale restrictions, excluding those
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to age-based resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Exemptions to appraisal underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages that are eligible for appraised value representation and warranty relief as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above, the Seller/Servicer is not responsible for underwriting the appraisal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure compliance with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Justification and support for the appraiser’s opinion of market value and how the final
elements.label: text

elements.text: value conclusion was determined. (See applicable language in Section 5605.7.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The validity of the appraiser’s adjustments in the analysis of comparable sales. (See
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable language in Section 5605.6.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the opinion of market value of the subject property is credible and adequately
elements.label: text

elements.text: supported. (See applicable language in Section 5605.1.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Exceptions to Loan Collateral Advisor appraised value representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even if a Mortgage meets the eligibility requirements above, the Seller/Servicer remains
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for compliance with the following requirements. Freddie Mac may still enforce
elements.label: text

elements.text: remedies if these requirements are not met, even if such matters are related to appraised
elements.label: text

elements.text: value:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must meet Freddie Mac’s property eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., not vacant, or undeveloped land, not primarily used for agricultural or farming
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes or for a commercial enterprise, etc.). (See Sections 5601.1 and 5701.2 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional details regarding property eligibility.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must not be subject to a pending legal proceeding for
elements.label: text

elements.text: condemnation in whole or in part (see Section 5601.1)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When applicable, Form 442, Appraisal Update and/or Completion Report, or Form 400,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Warranty of Completion of Construction or Repairs/Alterations, must be obtained to
elements.label: text

elements.text: verify that any outstanding conditions of the appraisal have been satisfied. (See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.8 for additional requirements on these forms.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must ensure the appraiser’s description of the subject property must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete and accurate (e.g., accuracy of quality and condition ratings, photographs that
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the quality and condition of the property, etc.). (See applicable language for
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal requirements in Chapter 5605.)

elements.text: 5602.3: Automated collateral evaluation (ACE)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Automated collateral evaluation (ACE) overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Process for qualifying for and accepting the ACE offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conditions that prevent the Seller from accepting an ACE offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acceptable age of the ACE offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data changes that impact ACE eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ACE requirements for Settlement Dates more than 120 days after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ACE eligibility in disaster areas
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller representation of property review or valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) ACE overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certain Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages, the Seller may receive the option to accept an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACE offer and originate the Mortgage without an appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller accepts the ACE offer, Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept the value of the Mortgaged Premises as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The purchase price of the Mortgaged Premises, for purchase transactions, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The estimated value of the Mortgaged Premises, for refinance transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not exercise its remedies, including the issuance of repurchase requests, in connection
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a breach of the Seller’s selling representations and warranties related to the value,
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition and marketability of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: For refinance transactions, the Sellers must have written procedures in place that prohibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes to the estimated value in Loan Product Advisor for the purpose of obtaining ACE or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more favorable mortgage terms (e.g., avoiding mortgage insurance).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The procedures must include a process for establishing the estimated value, which may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include, but is not limited to, the use of the Borrower’s estimated value, an Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Valuation Model (AVM) or an online valuation tool or website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Process for qualifying for and accepting the ACE offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage to qualify for an ACE offer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be an Accept Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Last Feedback Certificate must indicate the Mortgage is eligible for representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranty relief with ACE. (This represents the “offer.”)
elements.label: text
