elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty relief status is “Y” or “Yes”
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of accepting the ACE offer, the Seller may deliver the Mortgage with a PDR or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report if the eligibility requirements for ACE+ PDR or the selected appraisal type
elements.label: text

elements.text: are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: To accept the ACE offer, the Seller must deliver the Mortgage with the ULDD Data Points
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in the data delivery instructions in Section 6302.10(c).

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be secured by a 1-unit dwelling that is either a Primary Residence or a second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Meet the following occupancy and maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: home 90%

elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages are ineligible for ACE:

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Conditions that prevent the Seller from accepting an ACE offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may not accept the ACE offer if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An appraisal is required by law or regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from an ADU on a subject 1-unit Primary Residence is used to qualify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller is aware, based on a review of the sales contract, property disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement, property inspection, disclosure by the Borrower, etc., of an adverse physical
elements.label: text

elements.text: property deficiency that warrants a PDR or an appraisal report being obtained.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transaction Mortgages, the Seller must review the sales contract and, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided, the property disclosure statement, for adverse physical property deficiencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of an adverse physical property deficiency that warrants a PDR or an appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report being obtained include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A contaminated site or hazardous substance that affects the property or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Neighborhood in which the property is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The property has a deficiency that is consistent with a C5 or C6 condition rating (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: active roof leak(s), damaged or failing foundation). Refer to Section 5605.5(a) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: further information related to property condition.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5603.3(a) for further information on property disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Acceptable age of the ACE offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The ACE offer provided through the Loan Product Advisor Feedback Certificate message is
elements.label: text

elements.text: valid for 120 days. If the offer is more than 120 days old on the Note Date, resubmission to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor is required to determine ongoing ACE eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Data changes that impact ACE eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller changes loan data (e.g., address of the property, loan amount, purchase price,
elements.label: text

elements.text: estimate of value, loan type, property type, occupancy of the property, etc.) in a subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission, the original offer will become invalid, and Loan Product Advisor may provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: different ACE eligibility determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) ACE requirements for Settlement Dates more than 120 days after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Settlement Date is more than 120 days after the Note Date, the Seller must warrant that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the value of the subject property as of the Settlement Date is no less than the estimated value
elements.label: text

elements.text: or sales price submitted to Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) ACE eligibility in disaster areas
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may accept an ACE offer for properties located in disaster areas if the Seller can
elements.label: text

elements.text: represent and warrant that the value and marketability of the Mortgaged Premises has not
elements.label: text

elements.text: been adversely impacted. See Section 4407.1 for property condition requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Seller representation of property review or valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller that has accepted an ACE offer must not make any representation that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has performed a property review or obtained a valuation of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may begin delivering UAD 3.6-compliant appraisals prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this section.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept as the value of the Mortgaged Premises:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises

elements.label: text

elements.text: changes to the estimated value in Loan Product Advisor for the purpose of obtaining ACE+
elements.label: text

elements.text: PDR or more favorable Mortgage terms (e.g., avoiding mortgage insurance).

elements.label: text

elements.text: include, but is not limited to, the use of the Borrower’s estimated value, an Automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Valuation Model (AVM) or an online valuation tool or website.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: provided, the property disclosure statement, for adverse physical property deficiencies.

elements.label: text

elements.text: in which the property is located
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: active roof leak(s), damaged or failing foundation). Refer to Section 5605.5(a) for further
elements.label: text

elements.text: information related to property condition.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5603.3(a) for further information on property disclosure statements.

elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmission to Loan Product Advisor is required to determine ongoing ACE+ PDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Data changes that impact ACE+ PDR eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller changes loan data (e.g., address of the property, loan amount, purchase price,
elements.label: text

elements.text: estimate of value, loan type, property type, occupancy of the property, etc.) in a subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission, the original offer will become invalid, and Loan Product Advisor may provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: different eligibility determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) ACE+ PDR requirements for Settlement Dates more than 120 days after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Settlement Date is more than 120 days after the Note Date, the Seller must warrant that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the value of the subject property as of the Settlement Date is no less than the estimated value
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted to Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) ACE+ PDR eligibility in disaster areas
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgaged Premises is located in a disaster area, the Seller may accept an ACE+ PDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer if the Seller can represent and warrant that the value and marketability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises has not been adversely impacted. See Section 4407.1 for property
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Seller representation of property review or valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller that has accepted an ACE+ PDR offer must not make any representation that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has performed a property review or obtained a valuation of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) ACE+ PDR additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Mortgage delivered with a PDR must retain the PDR in the Mortgage file and also
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the additional requirements below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional PDR requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirement type Section reference
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5603.6
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Age of the PDR Section 5604.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: PDRs with required repairs and/or inspections Section 5605.9
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACE+ PDR upgrade to appraisal requirements Section 5604.1(d)
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 5603.1: Appraiser Independence Requirements, Seller
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: selection of appraisers and appraiser qualifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraiser Independence Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraiser and supervisory appraiser qualifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unlicensed and trainee appraisers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller representations and warranties regarding appraisers and appraisal reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representations by appraisers and appraisal management companies
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Seller responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complying with the Appraiser Independence Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selecting the appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requiring the appraiser to use the appropriate Freddie Mac appraisal report form
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complying with the Uniform Appraisal Dataset
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not select or approve individual appraisers or appraisal management
elements.label: text

elements.text: companies. The Seller, or a third party authorized by the Seller, is responsible for approving
elements.label: text

elements.text: and selecting the appraiser. The Seller warrants that the appraisal services provided comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice, applicable laws and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appraiser Independence Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each conventional Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac, the Seller represents and warrants that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal was obtained in accordance with Exhibit 35, Appraiser Independence
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Appraiser and supervisory appraiser qualifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser or supervisory appraiser must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be State-licensed or State-certified in the State in which the subject property is located,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have knowledge and experience in appraising the property type in the Market Area, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have access to applicable data sources
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Unlicensed and trainee appraisers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac permits unlicensed and trainee (or similar classification) appraisers to complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: an appraisal report in accordance with State law. If an appraisal report is completed by an
elements.label: text

elements.text: unlicensed or trainee (or similar classification) appraiser:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A supervisory appraiser must sign the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A supervisory appraiser is not required to inspect the subject property or comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales unless required by State law
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Seller representations and warranties regarding appraisers and appraisal reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to complying with the Appraiser Independence Requirements, with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: each appraisal report, the Seller represents and warrants that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All information known to the Seller that may affect the estimate of market value or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability has been provided to the appraiser. It has reviewed the appraisal report and
elements.label: text

elements.text: has determined the Mortgaged Premises is adequate collateral for the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction, as required by Section 4201.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report is of professional quality and supports all of the appraiser’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumptions, data, analyses, rationale and conclusions regarding the opinion of market
elements.label: text

elements.text: value of the property and the marketability of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report is credible, internally consistent, clearly written, fully supported and
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficiently documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deficient appraisals will be considered a breach of the Seller’s warranty regarding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptability of the Mortgage and will subject the Seller to the remedies available to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to reviewing the appraisal report submitted by the Seller, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: conduct property inspections and/or other investigations to verify property eligibility and
elements.label: text

elements.text: proper underwriting of the Mortgages offered for sale to and sold to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may refuse to accept appraisal reports prepared by a particular appraiser.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 3101.1 for additional requirements on the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Section 3101.2 for additional requirements on the FHFA Suspended Counterparty
elements.label: text

elements.text: Program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Representations by appraisers and appraisal management companies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisers and appraisal management companies must not make any representation to third
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties as being approved by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.label: text

elements.text: 5603.3: Information supplied to the appraiser and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Borrower (05/06/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information supplied to the appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information supplied to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Information supplied to the appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each appraisal report request, the Seller, or an authorized third party, must provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser with the following information about the subject property:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The complete legal description (refer to Section 5605.3)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The complete sales contract for purchase transactions, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All non-realty items
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financing terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financing and sales concessions granted by anyone associated with the transaction,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any gifts, buydowns or Down Payment assistance provided by anyone on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For new construction, the sales contract should state the base price of the house and
elements.label: text

elements.text: itemize each option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is not required to provide the appraiser with an updated sales contract unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the updated terms impact the physical description or condition of the property. In such
elements.label: text

elements.text: cases, the Seller must obtain an updated appraisal report for the property. Changes to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales contract that are not required to be provided to the appraiser include, but are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Changes to the transaction terms such as sales price, financing or sale concessions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date revisions, corrections to typographical errors, etc.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Any known affiliation between the property seller and the purchaser
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Income and expense statements and property leases
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Generally acceptable energy reports such as the Home Energy Rating System report and
elements.label: text

elements.text: U.S. Department of Energy Home Energy Score report, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Ground Lease for leasehold properties (refer to Section 5704.2)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Any other information known to the Seller, including information identified during
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s review of a property disclosure statement, that could adversely affect the market
elements.label: text

elements.text: value, condition or marketability of the subject property, including the existence of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contaminated Site, Hazardous Substance or other adverse condition that affects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property or the Neighborhood in which the subject property is located (refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5605.4(b)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A property disclosure statement (e.g., Residential Seller Disclosure Statement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer Disclosure Statement, Property Condition Disclosure Form, Seller’s Residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: Real Estate Disclosure) is completed by the property seller and covers general
elements.label: text

elements.text: information about the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property disclosure statement was provided to the Seller, the property disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement must be reviewed for conditions that could adversely affect the market value,
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition or marketability of the subject property. (Refer to Section 5605.5(a) for further
elements.label: text

elements.text: information related to property condition.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Information supplied to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transactions, the Seller must provide the Borrower with information regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: environmental hazards that directly impact the subject property and have not been mitigated
elements.label: text

elements.text: or remediated, unless the Borrower already has notice of such hazard(s) through the purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract or property inspection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If at any time before the Note Date the Seller becomes aware of a hazard that adversely
elements.label: text

elements.text: affects the market value, condition or marketability of the subject property, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: existence of a Contaminated Site, Hazardous Substance or other environmental condition that
elements.label: text

elements.text: has not been mitigated or remediated, the Seller must disclose to the Borrower all
elements.label: text

elements.text: information known to the Seller about the hazard.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5603.4: Unacceptable appraisal practices (01/26/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are examples of unacceptable appraisal practices. Evidence of any of the practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: listed in this section will be a breach of Seller’s warranty as to the professional quality of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Consideration of the race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital
elements.label: text

elements.text: status, disability, familial status, exercise of any federally protected civil right, receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: income derived from any public assistance program, birthplaces of residents at the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: or in the Neighborhood, national origin of the prospective owners or occupants of the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or of the present owners or occupants of the properties in the vicinity of the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property. (See also Section 1301.2 for equal opportunity compliance requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Use of unsupported or subjective terms or statements to assess or rate, such as, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, “high,” “low,” “good,” “bad,” “fair,” “poor,” “strong,” “weak,” “rapid,” “slow,”
elements.label: text

elements.text: “fast” or “average” without providing a foundation for analysis and contextual information
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Incorporating terminology or veiled language that could indicate underlying bias, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, “pride of ownership,” “crime-ridden area,” “desirable neighborhood or
elements.label: text

elements.text: location” or “undesirable neighborhood or location,” “gentrified,” “working class,” “inner
elements.label: text

elements.text: city,” “preferred community,” “up and coming,” predominantly Hispanic or Black
elements.label: text

elements.text: neighborhood, substantial amount of Black or Hispanic residents at the property, diverse
elements.label: text

elements.text: school system, amenities specifically geared to a race, ethnic or religious group or using
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms such as Millennials, Generation X or Baby Boomers
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Development of an appraisal using inaccurate or incomplete data about the subject property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Neighborhood, the Market Area or any comparable property used in the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis or report
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Inclusion of references, statements or comparisons about crime rates or crime statistics,
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether objective or subjective, in the appraisal analysis or report
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Consideration of the age or location of a dwelling, or the age of the Neighborhood or census
elements.label: text

elements.text: tract where the dwelling is located, in a manner that has a discriminatory effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Basing the development of an opinion of value on factors that local, State or federal law
elements.label: text

elements.text: designates as discriminatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Reliance in the appraisal analysis on comparable properties that were not personally
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspected by the appraiser when required by the appraisal’s scope of work. A personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection requires at least a visual inspection of the exterior of the comparable property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Reliance in any appraisal analysis on inappropriate comparable properties, or the failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: use comparable properties that are more similar, or nearer, to the subject property without
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate explanation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Use of comparable property data provided by any interested party to the transaction without
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification by a disinterested party
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Use of inordinate adjustments for differences between the subject property and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable properties that do not reflect the market’s reaction to such differences, or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to make proper adjustments when necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12. Development of an opinion of value and/or marketability conclusions that is not supported by
elements.label: text

elements.text: available market data
elements.label: text

elements.text: 13. Breach by the appraiser or supervisory appraiser of a certification or Statement of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assumptions and Limiting Conditions or comparable statements on any Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved appraisal report form or addendum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5606.3 for additional unacceptable appraisal practices related to electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal reports.

elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate explanation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Use of comparable property data provided by any interested party to the transaction without
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification by a disinterested party
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Use of inordinate adjustments for differences between the subject property and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable properties that do not reflect the market’s reaction to such differences, or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to make proper adjustments when necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12. Development of an opinion of value and/or marketability conclusions that is not supported by
elements.label: text

elements.text: available market data
elements.label: text

elements.text: 13. Breach by the appraiser or supervisory appraiser of a certification or Statement of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assumptions and Limiting Conditions or comparable statements on any Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5606.3 for additional unacceptable appraisal practices related to electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5603.5: Property Data Collector Independence
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Requirements and Seller engagement of property data
elements.label: text

elements.text: collectors (11/06/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Separation of functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Seller responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complying with the Property Data Collector Independence Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selecting the property data collector
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complying with the Uniform Property Dataset
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not select or approve individual property data collectors or property data
elements.label: text

elements.text: collector management companies. The Seller, or a third party authorized by the Seller, is
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for approving and selecting the property data collector. The Seller represents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants that the property data collection complies with Exhibit 42, Property Data Collector
elements.label: text

elements.text: Independence Requirements, applicable laws and Freddie Mac requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Separation of functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must maintain separation between its sales and Mortgage production functions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: its property data collection functions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5603.6: Property data collector qualifications and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Seller oversight (11/06/24)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property data collectors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller oversight of property data collectors
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property data collectors
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller, or its authorized third party, must ensure the property data collector:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is independent and unbiased and has no present or prospective interest or bias with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to the transaction, the property or the participants to the transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completes training in all aspects of property data collection and the Uniform Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UPD) and passes an exam that evidences proficiency in property data
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Performs all aspects of a property data collection including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Identifying and reporting, with supporting photographs, any:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Property characteristics represented in the UPD;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Design features that impair the use of the subject property and cannot easily be
elements.label: text

elements.text: changed (e.g., access to a bedroom only through another bedroom, etc.); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Factors adjacent to the subject property that are atypical for the location (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: power lines, commercial site, industrial site, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Measuring the dwelling unit(s) and any other structures on the subject property to
elements.label: text

elements.text: produce building sketches and floorplans in accordance with the exhibit requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 5604.2(a)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Collecting a comprehensive set of photographs of the subject property, as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the UPD. (See Section 5604.2(h) for photograph requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Seller oversight of property data collectors
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller, or its authorized third party, is responsible for the accuracy and reliability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: PDR and of any data provided by the property data collector to the appraiser. The Seller, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: its authorized third party, must have in place policies or procedures that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require property data collectors to undergo periodic background checks that include
elements.label: text

elements.text: criminal background checks;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require property data collectors to complete comprehensive training on preparing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate and thorough PDR;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require property data collectors to comply with the same customer service standards or
elements.label: text

elements.text: code of conduct with which employees and/or contractors of the Seller, or its authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: third party, must comply;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a process to continuously evaluate property data collectors, including monitoring
elements.label: text

elements.text: and documenting performance to identify and remedy any recurring deficiencies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: discontinuing the use of chronically underperforming property data collectors; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a process for providing continuing education when appropriate (e.g., in the event
elements.label: text

elements.text: of significant changes to the UPD, the property data collection process, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must include a targeted review of PDRs as part of its quality control sampling.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisals completed using a hybrid process must also be included in the Seller’s quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: sampling.
elements.label: text
